summary: Stellar Crazy Fingers→Let It Grow, soaring St. Stephen & tight second‑set flow
blurb: The Orpheum show delivers a standout Crazy Fingers → Let It Grow segue and a soaring Saint Stephen jam that rolls into Not Fade Away, Sugar Magnolia, Stella Blue and Sunshine Daydream. Jerry’s lead guitar string‑bending is spot‑on and his vocals shine, especially on the duet with Donna. The band moves tightly through both sets, making this a must‑listen 1976 performance.
review: On July 13, 1976 at San Francisco’s Orpheum Theatre the Dead were in top form. A relaxed first set builds from a solid Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo into a rapturous Crazy Fingers → Let It Grow sequence that showcases Jerry’s slippery Travis Bean tone and his “spot‑on” lead work, while the drums swell space‑filled behind the jam. The second set is where the night truly shines: Saint Stephen launches into an extended, soaring improvisation that seamlessly segues into Not Fade Away, then bursts through Sugar Magnolia, drifts into a beautifully rendered Stella Blue and resolves on Sunshine Daydream. Highlights also include High Time, The Music Never Stopped, Samson & Delilah and a joyous Dancin’ In The Street encore. Jerry’s vocal performance—highlighted by a duet with Donna—adds an extra layer of warmth, and Keith’s atmospheric chords on Stella Blue underscore the band’s tight, supportive interplay. Overall, this show captures the relaxed yet adventurous spirit of the 1976 theater era and stands out as one of the season’s most rewarding recordings.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six out of eight recordings are positive, praising the same standout songs and overall band tightness; only one mixed and one negative review temper the enthusiasm slightly, resulting in a strong but not maximal sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard capture with clear mix, minimal tape hiss and the fewest technical glitches; it highlights Crazy Fingers → Let It Grow and St. Stephen while preserving the band’s dynamic energy.

key_highlights: Crazy Fingers → Let It Grow segue and soaring Saint Stephen jam
Tight second‑set transitions linking Saint Stephen, Not Fade Away, Sugar Magnolia, Stella Blue, Sunshine Daydream
Jerry's vocal duet with Donna and his string‑bending lead guitar work

band_performance.Jerry: String‑bending and lead guitar are spot‑on; vocal performance, including a duet with Donna, is exceptional.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a deep, rich bass foundation that anchors the band’s improvisations.
band_performance.Bob: Locks in tightly with Donna, delivering solid rhythm work throughout.
band_performance.Keith: Atmospheric keyboard chords on Stella Blue and supportive playing across the set earn kudos.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums add spacey, swell‑filled textures especially inside Let It Grow; overall strong presence.
band_performance.Billy: Solid drumming that drives the jams and complements Mickey’s percussive colors.
